name: peac-mcp-server
description: Verify, inspect, decode, issue, and bundle PEAC receipts. Portable, offline-verifiable evidence for AI agent interactions.
long_description: PEAC Protocol provides cryptographically signed, offline-verifiable receipts that record what happened during automated interactions. Each receipt is a compact JWS (JSON Web Signature) using Ed25519 signatures. This MCP server exposes 5 tools for receipt operations: verify (signature + claims), inspect (metadata without verification), decode (raw JWS structure), issue (sign new receipts), and create_bundle (portable evidence directories).

tools.name: peac_verify
tools.description: Verify a PEAC receipt: check Ed25519 signature, validate claims, and return structured check results. Read-only, no side effects.

tools.name: peac_inspect
tools.description: Inspect a PEAC receipt without verifying the signature. Returns decoded header, payload metadata, and timestamps. Read-only.

tools.name: peac_decode
tools.description: Raw-decode a PEAC receipt JWS into header and payload objects. No signature check. Use peac_verify for trusted results.

tools.name: peac_issue
tools.description: Sign and return a new PEAC receipt JWS. Requires server to be configured with an Ed25519 issuer key (--issuer-key).

tools.name: peac_create_bundle
tools.description: Create a signed evidence bundle directory from one or more receipt JWS strings. Requires --issuer-key and --bundle-dir.

issuerKey.title: Issuer Key
issuerKey.description: Ed25519 JWK for receipt issuance (optional; read-only tools work without it). Pass as env:VAR or file:/path.

issuerId.title: Issuer ID
issuerId.description: Issuer URI, e.g. https://your-service.example.com. Required when issuerKey is set.
